summary: Chunk Rumi Masnavi Book I — regex-section-split on PROLOGUE/STORY [IVX]+
description:
Source: raw/sufism/masnavi-book-1.txt — 100,905 chars, single file (format=html, Whinfield 1898).

Audit findings:
- Head: sacred-texts crumb ('Sacred Texts Islam Index Next Buy this Book at Amazon.com'), then title block, then 'Book I. PROLOGUE. HEARKEN to the reed-flute...'.
- Tail: '*NOTES:' block (numbered footnotes 1, 2) then 'Next: The Masnavi Book II' trailer.
- Section markers are very clean: 'PROLOGUE', 'STORY I', 'STORY II', ..., 'STORY XIV' — all-caps on their own line.

Recommended strategy: regex-section-split.

pattern: '^(PROLOGUE|STORY [IVX]+)\b'

pre_strip:
  - leading sacred-texts crumb
  - trailing 'Next: The Masnavi Book II'

Keep '*NOTES' attached to its preceding chunk (the final STORY) — they belong to Story XIV per Whinfield's edition.
